title: puccinellia fasciculata
intro: Puccinellia fasciculata, also known as saltwort or sea sandwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the grass family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, while its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and topical applications. In various cultures, Puccinellia fasciculata has been used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory infections, and is often valued for its adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties.
